claudeMd:
# Task: Include hours and minutes in version timestamp with localization You are modifying a static HTML+JS Three.js game served by nginx. No npm, no build step. ## Codebase Pure static HTML+JS served by nginx. No npm, no build step. Three.js v0.183.2 via CDN importmap. - `index.html` — Main page with Three.js importmap, overlay UI, and all CSS - `js/main.js` — Game loop and state management - `js/physics.js` — Ball physics, collision detection - `js/renderer.js` — Three.js scene, mesh creation, level generation - `js/tracker.js` — MediaPipe head tilt detection (DO NOT MODIFY) - `Dockerfile` + `nginx.conf` — Docker build serving on port 8080 ## What to Change File: `index.html` (only file that needs changes) ### 1. Modify the `#version-info` div (line 296) Change: ```html
v1.0.0 · Last updated: 2026-03-20
``` To: ```html
v1.0.0
``` The `data-updated` attribute holds the ISO 8601 datetime string that developers update when deploying. The initial text is just the version number; the script below will append the formatted date. ### 2. Add an inline script right after the `#version-info` div (before the existing module script tag) Add this immediately after the version-info div: ```html ``` This uses the browser's built-in localization APIs to display the datetime in the user's locale and timezone. The `\u00b7` is the middle dot (·) character. ## Conventions - 2-space indent in HTML - Keep the `#version-info` CSS styling unchanged (bottom-right, unobtrusive, 0.7em font) - Use `var` in the inline script for maximum browser compatibility (this is NOT a module) - Do NOT modify any JS module files — this change is entirely in `index.html` - The ISO string in `data-updated` should include hours and minutes with UTC timezone indicator (`Z`) ## Gotchas - `toLocaleString(undefined, ...)` uses the browser's default locale — do NOT hardcode a locale string - The inline script must be a regular `

checks: The #version-info element in the bottom-right corner displays a localized timestamp that includes date, hours, and minutes (e.g., 'v1.0.0 · Updated: Mar 20, 2026, 08:30 PM' or equivalent in the browser's locale), not just a plain date like '2026-03-20'
The displayed datetime format uses the browser's native localization (toLocaleString), adapting to the user's locale and timezone
The version string text remains small, unobtrusive, and positioned in the bottom-right corner of the screen
The HTML source contains a data-updated attribute with an ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g., '2026-03-20T20:30:00Z') that developers can easily update
